Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::2	P10, Q5. All of the following may be useful in the treatment of cardiogenic shock except:	Dobutamine.	Sodium nitroprusside.	Pneumatic anti-shock garment. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::2	P10, Q8. Which of the following is/are true concerning the compensatory mechanisms and treatment of metabolic acidosis? C	Potassium replacement is essential even in the face of normal or high serum potassium when treating diabetic ketoacidosis. C	Increase in systemic vascular resistance.	<br>Venoconstriction is the primary mechanism to maintain preload (venous return) during hemorrhage; Angiotensin II is a potent vasoconstrictor of both arteries and veins. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::2	P16, Q38. Neurogenic shock is characterized by the presence of:	Cool moist skin.	Increased cardiac output.	Decreased blood volume.	Decreased peripheral vascular resistance.		D	Decreased peripheral vascular resistance.	Neurogenic shock involves loss of sympathetic tone, leading to massive vasodilation (decreased SVR) and warm, flushed skin.	Hypovolemic and Cardiogenic shock typically present with high SVR (vasoconstriction) and cool skin.	Treatment: Fluids and Vasopressors (Phenylephrine).	Surgery &gt; Shock

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::2	P16, Q40. Which of the following (if present) is a distinguishing feature of neurogenic shock?	Hypotension.	Bradycardia.	Vaso-dilation.	Vaso-constriction. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::2	P16, Q41. Male patient 59 years old, presented with diabetic foot wound during lab assessment before surgical debridement, the results show Na 137 mEq/L, K 6.5 mEq/L, random blood sugar 354 mg/dl, HB Alc 8.5 mg/dl. In this patient there is:	Hypokalemia.	Hyperkalemia.	Hyponatremia.	Hypernatremia.		B	Hyperkalemia.	Potassium (K) level of 6.5 mEq/L is elevated (Normal 3.5-5.0), indicating Hyperkalemia.	Hyperkalemia in diabetics is often due to insulin deficiency (which normally drives K into cells) and renal insufficiency.	Immediate treatment: Calcium gluconate (stabilize heart), Insulin+Glucose (shift K).	Surgery &gt; Fluids &amp; Electrolytes			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::2	P16, Q42. Rapid fluid resuscitation of the hypovolemic patient after abdominal trauma is significantly enhanced by which of the following? Bilateral saphenous vein cut downs.	placement of short, large-bore percutaneous peripheral intravenous catheters e- infusion of cold whole blood.		D	placement of short, large-bore percutaneous peripheral intravenous catheters e- infusion of cold whole blood.	"According to Poiseuille&#x27;s Law, flow is proportional to radius to the 4th power and inversely proportional to length; short, large-bore (14G/16G) peripheral IVs allow the fastest flow."	Central lines (long) have high resistance and are slower for volume resuscitation than peripheral large bore lines.	Avoid cold blood; warm fluids to prevent hypothermia and coagulopathy.	Surgery &gt; Trauma &amp; Critical Care			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::4	P33, Q1. Reactive proteins:	Is secreted in a circadian rhythm with higher levels in the morning.	Increase after eating a large meal. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::4	P42, Q60. Among the physiologic responses to acute injury is:	Increased secretion of insulin.	Increased secretion of thyroxine.	Decreased secretion of vasopressin.	Decreased secretion of aldosterone.		A	Increased secretion of insulin.	Wait, the provided key is (a). However, the classic response to injury is INSULIN RESISTANCE and HYPERGLYCEMIA, often with RELATIVE hypoinsulinemia or normal/high insulin that is ineffective. Catecholamines and Cortisol usually suppressing insulin or opposing it. BUT, insulin secretion generally INCREASES to combat the hyperglycemia, even if resistance exists. The other options are definitely wrong: Thyroxine is usually normal/low (Euthyroid sick), ADH INCREASES (conserve water), Aldosterone INCREASES (conserve Na). So (a) is the only plausible option.	Stress response: Increased Cortisol, Catecholamines, ADH, Aldosterone, Glucagon.	Hyperglycemia in stress is due to hepatic gluconeogenesis and peripheral insulin resistance.				> Endocrinology surgery

Complete transection of the CBD is best repaired with a Roux-en-Y hepatico/choledocho-jejunostomy to prevent stricture and reflux. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P17, Q2. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the cell function of phagocytosis? A	Phagocytosis is a mechanistically distinct process of endocytosis, performed by special cells to take up larger particles such as bacteria or erythrocytes. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P19, Q11. A 32-year-old man suffers a spinal cord injury with a resultant paraplegia in a motorcycle accident. C	Despite significant hypotension, secondary organ injury will be uncommon. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P19, Q12. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning septic shock?	The clinical picture of gram -ve septic shock is specifically different than shock associated with other infectious agents.	The circulatory derangements of septic shock precede the development of metabolic abnormalities.	Splanchnic vascular resistance falls in similar fashion to overall systemic vascular resistance.	Despite normal mechanism of intrinsic expansion of the circulating blood volume, exogenous volume resuscitation is necessary.		D	Despite normal mechanism of intrinsic expansion of the circulating blood volume, exogenous volume resuscitation is necessary.	Septic shock causes massive venodilation and capillary leak (relative hypovolemia), requiring large volume resuscitation (30ml/kg) despite the body's attempts to compensate.	Septic shock is Distributive Shock (High CO, Low SVR).	Early Goal Directed Therapy: Fluids first, then Vasopressors (Norepinephrine).				
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P20, Q13. Which of the following is/are true concerning the treatment of pulmonary interstitial edema?	Diuresis and blood transfusion is a valuable step.	Salt-poor albumin leaks through the capillaries and worsen the condition.	Mannitol is contraindicated as diuretics in this clinical situation.	Isoproterenol is a poor choice as an iono-tropic agent.		D	Isoproterenol is a poor choice as an iono-tropic agent.	Isoproterenol increases myocardial oxygen demand significantly and can cause arrhythmias, making it a poor choice compared to Dobutamine or Dopamine.	Pulmonary edema management: LMNOP (Lasix, Morphine, Nitrates, Oxygen, Position).	Albumin use in ARDS/Capillary leak is controversial as it may leak into interstitium.				
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P20, Q14. Which of the following is/are true concerning the assessment of protein reserve?	Conventional serum proteins such as albumin and globulin are early indicator of malnutrition.	The total lymphocyte count reflects patient immunity and not nutrition.	Antigen skin testing reflects patient immunity and not nutrition.	Measurement of urea excretion in urine can be used as a measurement of protein breakdown.		D	Measurement of urea excretion in urine can be used as a measurement of protein breakdown.	24-hour Urine Urea Nitrogen (UUN) allows calculation of nitrogen balance (Protein catabolism) to guide nutritional support.	Albumin has a long half-life (20 days) and is a poor marker of acute status. Prealbumin (2 days) is better.	Nitrogen Balance = (Protein intake / 6.25) - (UUN + 4).				
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P20, Q15. Narcotics are commonly used in administration of general anesthesia. Which of the following statement (s) is/are true concerning this class of agents? Naloxone should be used routinely for the reversal of narcotic analgesia.	Acutely injured hypovolemic patients are significant risk for decreased blood pressure with the use of narcotic analgesia.	Propofol is a new intra-venous short-acting narcotic used frequently in the outpatient setting.		D	Acutely injured hypovolemic patients are significant risk for decreased blood pressure with the use of narcotic analgesia.	الإجابة الصحيحة C والله أعلم<br>Opioids (narcotics) reduce sympathetic tone, which can precipitate severe hypotension in hypovolemic patients who rely on vasoconstriction.	Narcotics are NOT amnestic (Benzos/Propofol are). Propofol is NOT a narcotic.	Naloxone is for overdose, not routine reversal (causes pain crisis).

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P22, Q27. The cell type most characteristic of chronic inflammation is the:	Macrophage.	B cell.	Natural killer cell.	Neutrophil.	Eosinophil.	A	Macrophage. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P26, Q46. Which of the following is the most effective way to prevent post-operative seroma/infection in an obese patient after an open appendectomy? Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P27, Q47. Staphylococcus aureus produces each of the following Except:	Cell wall peptidoglycan.	Entero-toxin.	Epidermolytic-toxia.	Neuro-exotoxin. Neurotoxins target the nervous system; Staph toxins target tissues/GI.	Staph is Coagulase Positive.				
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P27, Q48. An exotoxin plays in important part in the pathogenicity of infection with each of the following Except:	Clostridium botulinum.	Clostridium tetani.	Escherichia coli.	Staphylococcus aureus.		C	Escherichia coli.	E. coli is a Gram-negative bacterium whose main virulence factor is Endotoxin (LPS) in the cell wall, not typically an Exotoxin (though ETEC/EHEC produce exotoxins, regular E. coli sepsis is LPS driven). The Clostridia and Staph rely heavily on Exotoxins.	Endotoxin = Lipid A of LPS (Gram negatives).	Exotoxins are secreted proteins (Gram positives and some negatives).

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P29, Q60. Steroids impair wound healing by:	Decreasing angio-genesis and macrophage migration.	Increasing release of lysosomal enzymes. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P29, Q63. Bradykinin, serotonin and histamine in wounds are released from:	Lymphocytes.	Mast cells.	Polymorpho-nuclear leukocytes.	Platelets.		B	Mast cells. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P29, Q64. Platelets in the wound form a hemostatic clot and release clotting factors to produce:	Fibrin.	Fibrinogen.	Thrombin.	Thromboplastin.		A	Fibrin. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P30, Q67. When a long bone fracture is repaired by internal fixation with plates and screws:	Callus at the fracture site forms more rapidly. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::3	P30, Q72. The tumor, node, metastases classification for melanoma uses the following in its classification Except:	Regional lymph nodes. Level II nodes are located posterior to the Pectoralis Minor muscle and include the central and interpectoral (Rotter's) nodes.	Level I: Lateral to Pectoralis Minor. Level III: Medial to Pectoralis Minor.	Surgical clearance usually involves Level I and II.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P63, Q9. The BRCA genes are:	A tumor suppressor gene.	Proto-onco genes.	Regulators of DNA transcription.	Regulators of protein modification.		A	A tumor suppressor gene.	BRCA1 and BRCA2 are tumor suppressor genes involved in DNA repair (homologous recombination). Mutations lead to chromosomal instability.	Inheritance is Autosomal Dominant.	BRCA1: Chromosome 17. BRCA2: Chromosome 13.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P63, Q10. The histological characteristic of Ductal Carcinoma In-situ is:	< Three cell layers protruding into the lumen of the duct.	< Five cell layers protruding into the lumen of the duct.	No invasion through the basement membrane.	Invasion into the blood vessel.		C	No invasion through the basement membrane.	DCIS is defined by malignant proliferation of ductal cells contained within the basement membrane, with no invasion into the stroma.	Once the basement membrane is breached, it becomes Invasive Ductal Carcinoma.	Comedo DCIS is a high-grade subtype with central necrosis.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P63, Q11. Anti-bodies in colostrum are produced by:	Duct epithelium.	Lactiferous sinus epithelium.	Lymphocytes.	Myo-epithelial cells.		C	Lymphocytes.	Secretory IgA and other antibodies in breast milk are produced by B-lymphocytes (plasma cells) that migrate to the breast from the gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT).	They provide passive immunity to the infant.	Myoepithelial cells contract to eject milk.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P63, Q12. Which statement best describes lymphatic drainage from the breast?	All quadrants of the breast drain into both the axillary and the para-sternal nodes.	The areolar area of the breast drains into retro-sternal internal mammary nodes.	The lateral area of the breast drains into axillary nodes.	The medial area of the breast drains into para-sternal nodes.		A	All quadrants of the breast drain into both the axillary and the para-sternal nodes.	While the majority (>75%) of drainage from all quadrants goes to the axilla, all quadrants also have potential drainage to the internal mammary (parasternal) nodes.	Axillary nodes are the primary site for metastasis.	Sentinel lymph node biopsy maps the specific drainage pattern for an individual patient.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P64, Q13. Drugs that may produce gynecomastia include all of the following Except:	Cimetidine.	Diazepam.	Furosemide.	Tamoxifen.		D	Tamoxifen.	Tamoxifen is a SERM (Selective Estrogen Receptor Modulator) that blocks estrogen effects in the breast and is used to TREAT gynecomastia, not cause it.	Cimetidine, Spironolactone, Digoxin, and some anti-anxiety meds (Diazepam) can cause gynecomastia.	Common mnemonic: 'Some Drugs Create Awesome Knockers' (Spironolactone, Digitalis, Cimetidine, Alcohol, Ketoconazole).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P64, Q14. Mammography is indicated for evaluation of a 40-year-old woman in all of the following situations Except:	Breast pain and discomfort in large fatty breasts without palpable nodules.	Breasts with multiple nodules but no dominant area.	Follow-up of the opposite breast after contralateral mastectomy for carcinoma,	Presence of a small, smooth, solitary lesion as a substitute for biopsy.		D	Presence of a small, smooth, solitary lesion as a substitute for biopsy.	Mammography is NOT a substitute for biopsy in a solid lesion. A tissue diagnosis (FNA or Core Biopsy) is required to rule out malignancy even if it appears benign.	Screening starts at age 40.	Diagnostic mammo is used for evaluation of lumps or pain.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P64, Q15. A 40-year-old woman is found to have a firm, smooth 3-cm mass in the upper outer quadrant of the breast. The mass is aspirated and 10ml of cloudy green fluid is removed. The appropriate management at this time is:	Cytologic evaluation of the aspirated fluid.	Administration of estrogen.	Excision of the cyst.	Observation only.		D	Observation only.	A simple cyst that disappears completely upon aspiration with non-bloody fluid (green/cloudy is typical) requires no further treatment, only observation.	Cytology is only indicated if the fluid is bloody or the mass persists.	If the mass recurs, re-aspiration or excision is indicated.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P64, Q16. Which of the following biopsy findings has the greatest relative risk for later carcinoma?	Apocrine metaplasia.	Atypical lobular hyperplasia.	Ductal ectasia.	Sclerosing adenosis.		B	Atypical lobular hyperplasia.	Atypical hyperplasia (ductal or lobular) confers a significant risk increase (4-5x) for future breast cancer.	Apocrine metaplasia and ductal ectasia are non-proliferative lesions with no increased risk.	Sclerosing adenosis has a mild risk (1.5-2x).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P64, Q17. A breast cancer that is 3 cm in diameter with positive, moveable, ipsilateral nodes is:	Stage I.	Stage II.	Stage III.	Stage IV.		B	Stage II.	A 3 cm tumor is T2 (2-5cm). Positive mobile axillary nodes are N1. T2N1M0 is Stage IIB.	Stage I is T1N0. Stage III involves fixed nodes (N2) or large tumors (T3/T4) with nodes.	TNM staging drives adjuvant therapy decisions.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P64, Q18. A 35-year-old woman undergoes her first screening mammogram. Which of the following mammographic findings would require a breast biopsy?	Breast calcifications larger than 2 mm in diameter.	Five or more clustered breast micro-calcifications per square centimeter.	A density that effaces with compression.	Multiple round well-circumscribed breast densities.		B	Five or more clustered breast micro-calcifications per square centimeter.	Clustered pleomorphic microcalcifications are highly suspicious for malignancy (DCIS) and mandate biopsy (stereotactic core).	Large calcifications (>2mm) are usually benign (popcorn calcifications of fibroadenoma).	Densities that disappear with compression are usually overlapping tissue (summation artifact).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P65, Q19. Estrogen receptor activity is clinically useful in predicting:	The presence of ovarian cancer.	The presence of metastatic disease. ER+ tumors respond well; ER- tumors do not.	It is also a prognostic factor (ER+ is generally less aggressive).	HER2 status predicts response to Trastuzumab.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P65, Q20. When galactorrhoea occurs in a high school girl, a diagnostic associated finding would be:	Gonadal Atrophy.	Bi-temporal Hemianopia.	Episodic Hypertension.	'Buffalo Hump'.		B	Bi-temporal Hemianopia.	Galactorrhea in a young woman suggests hyperprolactinemia, commonly from a pituitary adenoma (prolactinoma). Large adenomas compress the optic chiasm, causing bitemporal hemianopia.	Prolactinoma inhibits GnRH, leading to amenorrhea (not necessarily gonadal atrophy immediately).	MRI of the sella turcica is the diagnostic test.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P65, Q21. A 40-year-old woman have a 1to 2cm, slightly tender cystic mass in her breast; she has no axillary lymph-adenopathy. What course would you follow? However, aspiration is the correct first step for a cyst.	Cysts are common in perimenopausal women (40s).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P65, Q22. Fibro-cystic disease of the breast has been associated with elevated blood levels of:	Testosterone.	Progesterone.	Estrogen.	Luteinizing hormone.		C	Estrogen.	Fibrocystic changes are hormonally driven, often linked to relative estrogen dominance or imbalance between estrogen and progesterone. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P65, Q23. A 14-year-old black girl had her right breast removed because of a large mass. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P65, Q24. For pregnant women who are found to have breast cancer:	Termination of a first-trimester pregnancy is mandatory.	Carcinoma of the breast behaves more aggressively in pregnant women owing to hormonal stimulation.	Breast conservation is in-appropriate for 3rd-trimester pregnancies.	Administration of adjuvant chemo-therapy is safe for the fetus during the 2nd and 3rd trimesters.		C	Breast conservation is in-appropriate for 3rd-trimester pregnancies.	Breast conserving surgery requires post-op radiation, which is contraindicated in pregnancy. A	Intermittent thin or milky discharge can be physiologic.	Physiologic discharge is usually bilateral, multiductal, and milky/green. It happens with manipulation.	Pathologic discharge is spontaneous, unilateral, bloody/clear.	Bloody discharge is most commonly Intraductal Papilloma (benign), though cancer must be ruled out.				> Breast surgery

Is related to vitamin C intake.		A	Increases with increasing age.	Age is the single most important risk factor for breast cancer. The incidence rises continuously with age.	Dietary fat links are controversial/weak.	Gender is the other major risk factor (Female > Male).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P66, Q29. Paget's disease of the nipple.	Intra-ductal carcinoma.	Inflammatory carcinoma.	Sub-areolar mastitis.		B	Intra-ductal carcinoma.	The question asks for the pathologic cause (implying malignancy or specific lesion). While papilloma is the MOST common cause, if the options are malignancies, Intraductal carcinoma (DCIS/IDC) is the malignant cause of bloody discharge. The key selects (B).	Most common cause of bloody discharge: Intraductal Papilloma (Benign).	Most common malignant cause: DCIS.				> Breast surgery

'Infiltrating' by definition means invasive.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P67, Q32. Which of the following are the most important and clinically useful risk factors for breast cancer?	Fibrocystic disease, age, and gender.	Cysts, family history in immediate relatives, and gender.	Age, gender, and family history in immediate relatives.	Obesity, nulli-parity, and alcohol use.		C	Age, gender, and family history in immediate relatives.	The three strongest risk factors are Female Gender, Increasing Age, and Positive Family History (especially 1st degree relatives).	Genetic mutations (BRCA) are also critical.	Hormonal factors (nulliparity, late menopause) are significant but less potent than Age/Gender/Genetics.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::6	P67, Q33. Which of the following pathologic findings is the strongest contraindication to breast preservation (lumpectomy with breast radiation) as primary treatment for a newly diagnosed breast cancer? D	+ve surgical margin for invasive cancer.	Inability to achieve negative surgical margins is an absolute contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (BCT).	Extensive intraductal component (EIC) is a risk factor for recurrence but not an absolute contraindication if margins are clear.	Tumor size relative to breast size matters, but &gt;3cm alone is not a contraindication if the breast is large enough.				> Breast surgery

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::7	P73, Q8. Scar formation is part of the normal healing process following injury Which of the following tissues has the ability to heal without scar formation?	liver.	Skin.	Bone.	Muscle.		C	Bone.	Bone heals by regeneration (callus formation -&gt; remodeling to original Haversian structure), restoring original tissue architecture without fibrous scarring.	Other tissues (skin, muscle) heal by repair (fibrosis/scar).	Liver can regenerate (hypertrophy) but chronic injury leads to cirrhosis (fibrosis).				> Healing surgery Wound
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::7	P73, Q9. A32-year-old man suffers a spinal cord injury with a resultant paraplegia in a motorcycle accident. The low blood pressure can be assumed to be due to neurogenic shock.	The sole cause of hypotension is the loss of sympathetic input to the venous system.	Despite significant hypotension, secondary organ injury will be un common.	There is no role for pharmacological intervention to maintain blood pressure.		C	Despite significant hypotension, secondary organ injury will be un common. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::8	P79, Q29. Conservative management is generally recommended for patients with which of the following symptoms or signs of arterial insufficiency? Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::8	P79, Q30. Correct statements concerning anti-platelet therapy include:	Aspirin has been shown to be an effective anti-platelet agent.	Most anti-platelet agents work by enhancing prostaglandin synthesis.	Anti-platelet agents have not been shown to increase patency rates of coronary artery bypass grafts.	The anti-platelet effect of aspirin will last for the life of the platelet, which is generally 20-25 days.		A	Aspirin has been shown to be an effective anti-platelet agent.	Aspirin irreversibly inhibits cyclooxygenase (COX), preventing Thromboxane A2 formation and platelet aggregation.	Platelet lifespan is 7-10 days, not 20-25.	Aspirin improves patency of grafts and reduces cardiovascular events.	Surgery &gt; Pharmacology			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::8	P80, Q31. The sub-clavian steal syndrome is associated with which of the following hemodynamic abnormalities?	Venous congestion of upper extremities. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Arterial	P2, Q3. Emboli typically lodge at arterial bifurcations where the vessel diameter narrows abruptly (e.g., femoral bifurcation). Claudication distance is affected by all the following except:	Speed of walk	Degree of ischemia	Level of obstruction	Upstairs movement		C	Level of obstruction	Claudication distance is primarily determined by the balance of oxygen supply (degree of ischemia) and demand (speed, incline). The level of obstruction determines *where* the pain is, but the *distance* is a function of severity and effort.	Claudication is reproducible muscle pain relieved by rest.	"Step 1: Spinal stenosis causes &#x27;pseudoclaudication&#x27; which is positional, not strictly exertion-dependent."				> surgery Vascular

Normal venous filling time is 10-15 seconds.	"Step 2 CK: Buerger&#x27;s test (dependent rubor, elevation pallor) is a bedside clinical sign of severe ischemia."				> surgery Vascular

Rest pain typically occurs at night when the patient is supine and cardiac output/gravity assistance is lower.	Step 2 CK: Patients often report sleeping in a chair to dangle legs for pain relief.				> surgery Vascular

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Arterial	P6, Q33. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Arterial	P7, Q40. The most serious complication of an abdominal aortic aneurysm is:	Thrombosis	Distal emboli	Retro-peritoneal rupture	Intra-peritoneal rupture		D	Intra-peritoneal rupture	Intraperitoneal rupture leads to exsanguinating hemorrhage into the large peritoneal cavity, resulting in rapid death. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Arterial	P7, Q43. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Arterial	P8, Q46. Local heat is useful for the following except:	Ischemic pain relief.	Relief of muscle spasm	Improving local circulation.	Sedation.	Resolution of inflammatory edema.	A	Ischemic pain relief.	Applying local heat to an ischemic limb is dangerous. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Arterial	"P8, Q47. The most important prognostic sign of &#x27; acute ischaemia of a limb is"	Pallor.	Cold skin.	cutaneous anaethesia.	Muscular paralysis. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::9	P84, Q1. The overall of indirect to direct inguinal hernia is:	2:1 (indirect direct).	1:1 (indirect direct).	1:2 (indirect direct).	1:3 (indirect direct). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ كتاب القسم::9	P85, Q9. The following statement about the repair of inguinal hernias are true Except:	"The conjugated tendon is sutured to Cooper&#x27;s ligament in the Bassini&#x27;s hernia repair."	The Mc Vay repair is a suitable option for repair of femoral hernias.	The Shouldice repair involves a multi-layer, imbricated repair of the floor of the inguinal canal.	The Lichtenstein repair is accomplished by prosthetic mesh repair of the inguinal canal floor in tension-free manner.	The laparoscopic trans-abdominal pre-peritonial (TAPP) and totally extra-peritonial approach (TEPA) repair are based on the pre-peritonial repairs of cheattle, Henry, Nyhus, and Stoppa.	A	"The conjugated tendon is sutured to Cooper&#x27;s ligament in the Bassini&#x27;s hernia repair."	"In the Bassini repair, the conjoined tendon is sutured to the INGUINAL LIGAMENT (Poupart&#x27;s), not Cooper&#x27;s ligament. Cooper&#x27;s ligament is used in the McVay repair."	McVay repair is essential for Femoral Hernias (closes femoral ring).	Lichtenstein (Tension-free mesh) is the current gold standard for open repair.				> Hernia surgery

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P2, Q1. The most useful investigation for a breast with a prosthesis is:	Mammography.	US.	FNABC.	MRI.	Hormonal receptors.	D	MRI.	MRI is the most sensitive imaging modality for evaluating breast implants (prostheses) and detecting rupture or intracapsular leaks, as mammography is often obscured by the implant.	MRI has the highest sensitivity for silicone implant rupture (linguine sign).	Step 2 CK: Screening mammography is still performed in women with implants, but special displacement views (Eklund views) are required.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P2, Q2. Triple assessment include the following except:	Clinical evaluation.	Imaging.	Laboratory investigations.	Cytology.		C	Laboratory investigations.	"The &#x27;Triple Assessment&#x27; for a breast lump consists of: 1. Clinical examination (palpation), 2. Imaging (Mammogram/US), and 3. Pathology (FNAC or Core Biopsy). General lab investigations are not part of the diagnostic triad for a lump."	Concordance of all three components (benign or malignant) yields &gt;99% diagnostic accuracy.	Step 2 CK: If any component of the triple assessment is discordant or suspicious, excisional biopsy is indicated.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P2, Q3. Etiology of breast abscess include:	Mostly in lactating women.	Due to Staph from baby mouth.	Bad hygiene.	All of the above.		D	All of the above.	"Lactational breast abscesses are most common, typically caused by Staphylococcus aureus transmission from the infant&#x27;s mouth through nipple fissures, often exacerbated by poor hygiene or stasis." Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P2, Q4. Signs of pus formation are the following except:	Hectic fever.	Edema of overlying skin.	No response to medical treatment for 48 hours.	Fluctuation occurs early in breast abscess.		D	Fluctuation occurs early in breast abscess.	Fluctuation is often a late sign in breast abscesses because the breast tissue is compartmentalized by fibrous septa and the abscess is often deep. Relying on fluctuation delays diagnosis.	Deep breast abscesses may not fluctuate; localized induration and point tenderness are earlier signs.	Step 2 CK: Ultrasound is the diagnostic modality of choice to differentiate cellulitis/mastitis from a drainable abscess.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P2, Q5. Best treatment of chronic breast abscess is:	Prolonged antibiotic courses.	Repeated aspiration.	Incision and drainage.	Excision of the whole abscess.		D	Excision of the whole abscess.	Chronic breast abscesses typically have thick walls and fibrotic cavities that do not collapse with drainage; excision of the abscess cavity (saucerization or complete excision) is often required for cure.	Chronic abscesses may be associated with duct ectasia or foreign body reactions and often require histological rule-out of malignancy. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P2, Q6. Clinical findings of fibroadenosis include:	Cyclic pain.	Cyclic swelling.	Cyclic discharge.	All of the above.		D	All of the above.	Fibroadenosis (Fibrocystic change) is characterized by physiological responses to hormonal cycles, leading to premenstrual pain (mastalgia), generalized lumpiness/swelling, and occasionally cyclical nipple discharge.	Most common benign breast condition, peaking in women aged 30-50.	Step 1: Histology shows cysts, fibrosis, and adenosis (blue dome cysts).				> Breast surgery

Blue-domed cysts are a non-proliferative fibrocystic change and carry no increased risk of cancer.	Step 1: Non-proliferative changes (cysts, apocrine metaplasia, fibrosis) do not increase cancer risk.				> Breast surgery

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P3, Q11. Bleeding on zonal pressure is highly suggestive of:	Breast abscess.	Duct papilloma.	Cancer breast.	Cystosarcoma phylloides.		B	Duct papilloma.	Intraductal papilloma is the most common cause of bloody nipple discharge. Applying pressure to a specific zone (trigger point) often elicits the discharge, localizing the affected duct.	Classic presentation: Unilateral, spontaneous, bloody discharge from a single duct.	Step 2 CK: Workup includes mammogram and ultrasound; definitive diagnosis/treatment is microdochectomy (duct excision).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P3, Q12. Best treatment for duct papilloma is:	Follow up.	Local excision.	Local excision with safety margin.	Chemotherapy and/or radiation.		C	Local excision with safety margin.	Microdochectomy (excision of the affected duct) is curative and diagnostic. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P3, Q13. The following is treatment of choice in pericanalicular fibroadenoma:	Irradiation.	Enucleation.	Removal en block.	Simple mastectomy.		B	Enucleation.	Fibroadenomas are encapsulated benign tumors. The standard surgical treatment, if indicated (for size or patient anxiety), is simple enucleation (shelling out) through a cosmetically placed incision. Step 2 CK: In women &lt; 30 with a clinically benign mass, observation or ultrasound is first line; biopsy if growing or suspicious.				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P3, Q14. Probe test can differentiate cystosarcoma phylloides from:	Fibroadenosis.	Breast mouse.	Cancer breast.	Chronic breast abscess.		C	Cancer breast.	Cystosarcoma phylloides (Phyllodes tumor) is not attached to the skin or deeper structures initially, unlike advanced breast carcinoma which fixes to tissues. A probe test (or clinical mobility check) demonstrates this lack of infiltration.	Phyllodes tumors are large, fast-growing, but usually mobile (non-infiltrative borders compared to scirrhous carcinoma). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P4, Q15. Risk factors of cancer breast include all the following except:	Mutation in suppressor genes (BRCA I, II).	Early menarche.	Carcinoma in situ.	Prolonged lactation.	White races.	D	Prolonged lactation.	Prolonged lactation is a protective factor against breast cancer (reduces lifetime estrogen exposure). Early menarche, genetic mutations, and history of carcinoma in situ are established risk factors.	Breastfeeding decreases the risk of breast cancer, particularly in premenopausal women.	Step 2 CK: The strongest risk factors are gender (female), age, and genetic mutations (BRCA).				> Breast surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P4, Q16. Increased occurrence of cancer breast in upper lateral quadrant is due to:	Increased estrogen receptors.	Increased breast mass. The Upper Outer Quadrant (Tail of Spence) contains the largest volume of glandular breast tissue and is the most common site for carcinoma (approx 50%). Some theories suggest receptor density varies, but tissue volume is the primary driver.	Anatomical fact: The majority of the mammary gland tissue is located in the upper outer quadrant. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P4, Q19. The cause in skin dimpling in any fibrotic condition of the breast is the affection of:	Milk duct..	Milk acini.	"Cooper&#x27;s ligament." Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Breast	P5, Q22. All the following are recognized side effects of radiotherapy except:	Local burn.	Pulmonary fibrosis.	End arteritis.	None of the above. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P1, Q5. In hemorrhagic shock peripheral vascular resistance:	Increase.	Decrease.	Remain constant.	Any of the above.		A	Increase.	In hemorrhagic (hypovolemic) shock, the body compensates for volume loss by intense sympathetic vasoconstriction, significantly increasing peripheral vascular resistance to maintain blood pressure.	Compensation phases: 1. Tachycardia, 2. Increased SVR, 3. Hypotension (decompensation).	Step 1: Baroreceptors in the carotid sinus detect low pressure and trigger sympathetic outflow.				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P1, Q6. The incriminated organism in septic shock may be:	gram +ve bacteria.	Gram -ve bacteria.	Candida albicans.	Any of the above.		D	Any of the above.	Septic shock can be caused by Gram-negative bacteria (endotoxins), Gram-positive bacteria (exotoxins/superantigens), or fungi (Candida). Gram-negative organisms are historically the most frequent cause, but incidence of Gram-positive sepsis is rising.	Endotoxin (Lipopolysaccharide/LPS) from Gram-negative cell walls is a potent trigger of the inflammatory cascade (TNF-alpha, IL-1).	Step 1: Lipid A is the toxic component of LPS.				> Shock surgery

Step 1: Cell membrane Na+/K+ ATPase maintains the gradient (High K+ in, High Na+ out).				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P3, Q16. Interstitial water is about:	10kg.	20kg.	25kg.	28kg.		A	10kg.	Extracellular Fluid (ECF) is 20% of body weight (14kg). ECF consists of Interstitial Fluid (3/4) and Plasma (1/4). 3/4 of 14kg is ~10.5kg.	Interstitial fluid is an ultrafiltrate of plasma, lacking proteins.	Step 1: Edema is an expansion of the interstitial fluid compartment.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P3, Q17. Iltravascular body water is about:	1kg.	2.5kg.	3.5kg.	5kg.		C	3.5kg.	Intravascular fluid (Plasma) represents 1/4 of the Extracellular Fluid (ECF) or 5% of body weight. 5% of 70kg is 3.5kg.	Total Blood Volume (Plasma + Cells) is ~7% of body weight (5L).	Step 1: Plasma volume can be measured using radiolabeled albumin or Evans blue dye.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P3, Q18. The highest concentration of K+ occurs in:	Urine.	Succus entericus.	Semen.	Saliva.		B	Succus entericus.	"The key selects &#x27;Succus entericus&#x27; (intestinal juice). While salivary K+ is high relative to plasma, distal intestinal secretions and stool have very high K+ concentrations due to colonic secretion, leading to significant hypokalemia in diarrhea."	Diarrhea causes normal anion gap metabolic acidosis with hypokalemia.	Step 1: Aldosterone acts on the colon (and kidney) to secrete K+.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P3, Q19. The following conditions lead to hypokalemia except:	Thiazide intake.	Cushing $.	Spirolctone intake.	Liver cell failure.		C	Spirolctone intake.	"Spironolactone is a potassium-sparing diuretic (Aldosterone antagonist), causing Hyperkalemia, not Hypokalemia. Thiazides and Cushing&#x27;s (excess cortisol/mineralocorticoid effect) cause potassium wasting."	Hyperaldosteronism -&gt; Na+ retention, K+ and H+ loss (Alkalosis).	Step 1: Spironolactone acts at the cortical collecting duct.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P3, Q20. In acute respiratory alkalosis bicarbonate level:	Increases.	Decreases.	Remains constant.	Any of the above.		C	Remains constant.	In *acute* respiratory alkalosis, the renal compensation (excreting bicarbonate) has not yet occurred (takes 24-48 hours). Therefore, bicarbonate levels remain initially normal/constant, though pH increases.	Acute compensation involves intracellular buffering, not renal changes.	Step 1: Chronic respiratory alkalosis will show decreased bicarbonate.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P3, Q21. Hypernatraemia can present by:	Irritability.	Twitches.	Convulsions.	Hyperreflexia.	Any of the above.	E	Any of the above.	Hypernatremia causes cellular dehydration (neuronal shrinkage), leading to CNS excitability signs: irritability, twitching, hyperreflexia, seizures, and coma.	Rapid correction of hypernatremia causes cerebral edema.	Step 2 CK: Calculate Free Water Deficit to guide correction rate (max 10-12 mEq/L/day).				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P4, Q22. Most common cause of water toxicity is:	latrogenic.	Compulsive water intake.	Increase ADH.	None of the above.		A	latrogenic.	Water toxicity (Hyponatremia) in surgical patients is most commonly iatrogenic, due to the administration of excessive hypotonic fluids (like D5W) in the setting of stress-induced ADH release.	Post-operative pain and nausea stimulate ADH, impairing free water excretion.	Step 2 CK: Acute hyponatremia (&lt;48h) can be treated more aggressively than chronic to prevent herniation.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P4, Q23. The most urgent measure in the management of a severely injured patient in the reception roam is:	Control of active bleeding.	Taking blood sample for grouping and cross matching.	Establishing an intravenous line.	Securing clear airway and adequate pulmonary ventilation.	providing tetanus prophylaxis.	D	Securing clear airway and adequate pulmonary ventilation.	"According to ATLS protocol, &#x27;A&#x27; (Airway) takes precedence over &#x27;B&#x27; (Breathing) and &#x27;C&#x27; (Circulation). Securing the airway is the first priority in trauma resuscitation."	ABCDE: Airway, Breathing, Circulation, Disability, Exposure.	Step 2 CK: If the patient speaks, the airway is patent. If unconscious/GCS&lt;8, intubate.				> surgery trauma
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P4, Q24. Oligemic shock is/Characterized by	Tachycardia.	Hypotension.	Pallor.	Collapsed veins.	Generalized vasoconstriction.	E	Generalized vasoconstriction.	Oligemic (Hypovolemic) shock triggers a massive sympathetic response causing generalized peripheral vasoconstriction to shunt blood to vital organs (Brain and Heart). Note: Tachycardia etc. are also present, but generalized vasoconstriction is the defining physiologic state distinguishing it from distributive shock.	Vasoconstriction leads to cool, clammy skin (Cold Shock).	Step 1: Angiotensin II and Vasopressin contribute to the vasoconstriction.				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P4, Q25. The essential pathophysiological disorder in all types of shock is	Hypotension.	Tachycardia.	Viscerocutaneous vasoconstriction.	Impaired tissue perfusion.	Reduced blood volume.	D	Impaired tissue perfusion.	Shock is defined as a state of inadequate tissue perfusion (oxygen delivery) to meet metabolic demands, leading to cellular hypoxia. Hypotension is a late sign, not the definition.	Cellular hypoxia leads to anaerobic metabolism and lactic acidosis.	Step 1: Lactate is a marker of tissue hypoperfusion.				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P4, Q26. The hyperdynamic phase of septic shock is characterized by the following signs except:	Hyperventilation.	Hypotension.	Tachycardia.	pale cold extermities.	Oliguria.	D	pale cold extermities.	"The hyperdynamic (Warm) phase of septic shock is characterized by high cardiac output and vasodilation, leading to warm, flushed extremities. &#x27;Pale cold extremities&#x27; occur in the hypodynamic (Cold) phase or hypovolemic shock."	Septic shock: Low SVR, High CO (initially). Hypovolemic/Cardiogenic: High SVR, Low CO.	Step 2 CK: Warm shock is unique to distributive shock (sepsis, anaphylaxis, neurogenic).				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P4, Q27. Septic shock responds best to	Massive antibiotics.	Intravenous infusion.	Adrenocortical steroids.	Drainage of septic collections.	Vasopressors.	D	Drainage of septic collections.	"&#x27;Source Control&#x27; (drainage of abscesses, debridement of necrotic tissue) is the most critical definitive step in treating septic shock. Antibiotics and fluids support the patient but cannot cure sepsis if the source (pus under pressure) remains."	"Principle: &#x27;Ubi pus, ibi evacua&#x27; (Where there is pus, let it out)."	Step 2 CK: Resuscitation (fluids/pressors) + Antibiotics + Source Control is the triad of sepsis management.				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P5, Q28. A comatose patient who has sustained multiple closed injuries is admitted with severe hypotension. The hypotension is most probably due to :	Intracranial hematoma.	Cerebral concussion.	Internal hemorrhage.	Neurogenic shock.	Cardiogenic shock.	C	Internal hemorrhage.	In trauma, hypotension is hypovolemic (hemorrhagic) until proven otherwise. Isolated head injury (intracranial hematoma) does NOT cause hypotension in adults (the skull cannot hold enough blood to cause shock).	"Cushing&#x27;s Triad (Bradycardia, Hypertension, Irregular respiration) is seen in raised ICP, not hypotension."	Step 2 CK: Hypotension in a trauma patient with a head injury suggests bleeding in the Chest, Abdomen, or Pelvis/Long bones.				> surgery trauma
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P5, Q29. Trauma induces increased secretion of the following hormones except:	Insulin.	Catecholamines.	ACTH and cortisone.	ADH (antidiuretic hormone).	Growth hormone.	A	Insulin.	The metabolic response to trauma (stress response) involves a catabolic state with suppression of insulin and elevation of counter-regulatory hormones (Catecholamines, Cortisol, Glucagon, GH) to mobilize glucose (hyperglycemia).	"Trauma causes insulin resistance and hyperglycemia (&#x27;stress diabetes&#x27;)."	Step 1: Epinephrine inhibits insulin secretion via alpha-adrenergic receptors.				> surgery trauma
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P5, Q30. The best guide for the required blood transfusion in haemorrhagic shock is the:	Arterial B.P.	Pulse rate.	Haematocrit.	Central venous pressure (CVP)	Urine output per minute.	D	Central venous pressure (CVP)	CVP (or more accurately, response to fluid challenge monitoring preload) is a better guide for volume status/replacement than BP or Pulse (which can be compensated) or Hematocrit (which lags behind acute loss). *Note: Urine output is also a key clinical indicator.*	CVP measures right atrial pressure, reflecting intravascular volume status.	Step 2 CK: In modern practice, dynamic parameters (Pulse Pressure Variation) are superior to static CVP.				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P5, Q31. In the adult respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) due to shock lung, the most important therapeutic measure is:	Oxygen inhalation.	Mechanical ventilation.	Large doses of methyl prednisolone.	Massive antibiotics.	Intravenous diuretics.	B	Mechanical ventilation.	ARDS causes severe hypoxemia refractory to oxygen alone. Mechanical ventilation with Positive End-Expiratory Pressure (PEEP) is essential to recruit collapsed alveoli and improve oxygenation.	Pathology: Diffuse alveolar damage, hyaline membranes, non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema.	Step 2 CK: Use Low Tidal Volume ventilation (6mL/kg) to prevent barotrauma in ARDS.				> Shock surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P5, Q32. Concerning post-operative wound infection, which of the following statements is wrong?	Is most often due to dead space.	Can always be prevented by prophylactic antibiotics.	Frequently takes the form of stitch abscess.	May precipitate fatal secondary hemorrhage.	May end fatally by septic shock.	B	Can always be prevented by prophylactic antibiotics.	Prophylactic antibiotics reduce risk but cannot *always* prevent infection, especially if surgical technique is poor (hematoma, dead space) or contamination is heavy. They are an adjunct, not a guarantee.	Prophylaxis should be given &lt;60 mins before incision and stopped &lt;24h post-op.	Step 2 CK: Most common cause of SSI is Staphylococcus aureus.				> infection surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P5, Q33. Which statement is wrong concerning Clostridriurn tetani?	Is a gram-negative bacillus.	Has a drum-stick appearance.	Is a strict anaerobe.	Produces highly resistant spores.	Secretes a powerful neuro-exotoxin.	A	Is a gram-negative bacillus.	Clostridium tetani is a Gram-positive, spore-forming, anaerobic rod. Step 1: Tetanospasmin toxin cleaves SNARE proteins, blocking release of inhibitory neurotransmitters (GABA/Glycine).				> infection surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P6, Q34. The prodromal symptoms of tetanus include the following except:	Restlessness and sleeplessness. High fever is typically a sign of severe, established autonomic instability or secondary infection in tetanus, not a prodromal (early warning) symptom. Early signs are trismus, local stiffness, and irritability.	First symptom is usually Trismus (Lockjaw). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P6, Q36. The most lethal exotoxin of, Cl. welchii is:	Hyaluronidase.	Collagenase.	Haemolysin.	Lecithinase (alpha toxin).	E. Deoxyribonuclease.	D	Lecithinase (alpha toxin). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P6, Q37. Which antibiotics are contraindicated in pregnant females:	Penicillins.	Cephalosporins.	Tetracyclines.	Aminoglycosides.	Lincosamines.	C	Tetracyclines.	Tetracyclines are contraindicated in pregnancy because they chelate calcium, depositing in fetal teeth and bones, causing discoloration and inhibition of bone growth. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P6, Q38. Most hands infections are caused by:	Streptococci.	Staphylococci.	E. Coli.	Anaerob.es.	Pseudomonas.	B	Staphylococci. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P6, Q39. The hand infection which carries the highest risk of developing osteomyelitis is :	paronychia. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P7, Q40. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P7, Q42. Congenital dermoid cysts are characterized by the following features (except) that they:	Occur at lines of embryological fusion. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P7, Q43. Sebaceous cyst is characterized by the following except that it:	Is due to obstruction of a sebaceous gland. D	May occur on the palms and soles	Sebaceous cysts (epidermal inclusion cysts) cannot occur on the palms and soles because these areas lack sebaceous glands/hair follicles. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P7, Q44. A patient was operated upon under general anaesthesia at 9 a.m. Suddenly at 8 p.m. on the same day, he developed fever of 390C. Very early post-operative fever (within 24-48 hours) is classically attributed to Atelectasis (collapse of alveoli), though physiological metabolic response is also a cause. Wound infection usually appears Day 3-5.	"The 5 W&#x27;s of Post-op Fever: Wind (Atelectasis), Water (UTI), Wound, Walk (DVT), Wonder drugs." Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P7, Q45. Among the ABO blood groups, agglutinogen A is absent from the cells of:	Group A.	Group B.	Group AB.	All of the above.	None of the above.	B	Group B.	Blood Group B has B antigens (agglutinogens) on RBCs and Anti-A antibodies in plasma. Therefore, Agglutinogen A is absent. Group O also lacks A antigen.	Group AB has both A and B antigens (Universal Recipient).	Step 1: ABO incompatibility causes acute hemolytic transfusion reaction (IgM mediated).				> general surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P8, Q46. Banked blood is deficient in all of the following except:	Platelets.	White cells.	Oxygen-carrying capacity of Hb.	Potassium.	Anti-haemophilic globulin.	D	Potassium.	Stored blood is deficient in viable platelets, WBCs, and labile clotting factors (V and VIII). However, potassium levels *increase* due to leakage from RBCs during storage (lysis/leak). Therefore, it is NOT deficient in K+; it has excess K+.	Massive transfusion can cause Hyperkalemia and Citrate toxicity (Hypocalcemia).	Step 2 CK: 2,3-DPG levels decrease in stored blood, shifting the Oxygen dissociation curve to the Left (holding onto O2).				> general surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P8, Q47. The most serious complication of blood transfusion is:	Pyrogenic reactions.	Thrombophlebitis of recipient vein.	Circulatory overloading.	Incompatibility reactions.	Viral hepatitis.	D	Incompatibility reactions.	Acute hemolytic transfusion reaction (ABO incompatibility) is the most life-threatening acute complication, causing shock, DIC, and renal failure.	Symptoms: Fever, flank pain, hypotension, dark urine.	Step 2 CK: First step if reaction suspected: STOP the transfusion.				> general surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P8, Q48. Concerning potassium depletion, the incorrect statement among the following is:	Follows loss of gastrointestinal secretions.	May be produced by diuretics.	Is often associated with acidosis.	Predisposes to cardiac arrhythmias.	Produces severe muscular weakness.	C	Is often associated with acidosis.	Hypokalemia is typically associated with *Alkalosis* (intracellular H+ shift out, K+ shift in, and renal H+ secretion). Hyperkalemia is associated with Acidosis. (Exception: RTA).	Clinical features: Muscle weakness, Ileus, U waves on ECG.	Step 1: K+ and H+ exchange across cell membranes maintains electrical neutrality.				> Fluids surgery
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P8, Q49. Acidosis may be produced by the following except:	Prolonged shock.	Hypoventilation.	Uncontrolled diabetes.	Open heart surgery.	Cirrhosis of the liver.	E	Cirrhosis of the liver.	Liver cirrhosis typically causes respiratory alkalosis (central stimulation) and metabolic alkalosis (secondary hyperaldosteronism/diuretics). It does not primarily cause acidosis unless in terminal renal failure. Shock (Lactic), Hypoventilation (Respiratory), Diabetes (Ketoacidosis) all cause acidosis.	Acidosis: pH &lt; 7.35. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P8, Q50. The earliest sign of hypocalcaemia is	Carpopedal spasms.	"Positive Chvostek&#x27;s sign."	"Positive Trousseau&#x27;s sign." Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P9, Q55. Among the following statements about branchial fistula, the incorrect one is that it:	Commonly results from rupture of a branchial cyst. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P9, Q57. The most serious post-operative chest complications is	Pulmonary atelectasis.	Bronchopneumonia.	Pulmonary embolism.	Lung abscess. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::General	P10, Q58. The untrue statement about desmoid tumors of the abdominal wall is that they:	Arise from the rectus muscle or its sheath. "Stemmer&#x27;s sign (inability to pinch the skin on the dorsum of the second toe) is pathognomonic for lymphedema."	Step 2 CK: Lymphedema is painless and does not ulcerate initially, distinguishing it from venous stasis.				> Lymphatics surgery

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Lymphatics	P2, Q12. About aspiration of cold abscess, all of the following is true except:	Dependant site	Z-technique	Complete aspiration	Injection of streptomycin solution		A	Dependant site	Aspiration of a cold abscess should be performed through *non-dependent*, healthy skin using a Z-track technique to prevent leakage and sinus tract formation. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Lymphatics	P4, Q26. In a motorcycle accident, a young male sustained a posterior dislocation of his right knee. After reduction of the dislocation, the ankle pulses were intact and the limb was immobilized in a splint. Six hours later, he complained of pain in the right leg with loss of sensation in the foot. The proper initial management is	Observation for frank signs of ischaemia.	Administration of anticoagulants and vasodilators.	X-ray examination of the lumbosacral spine.	Femoral arteriography.	Fasciotamy to relieve tension.	D	Femoral arteriography.	Knee dislocation carries a high risk of Popliteal Artery injury. Even if pulses are initially present, intimal flaps can cause delayed thrombosis. Pain and sensory loss indicate ischemia. Immediate imaging (Arteriography/CTA) is required to confirm the lesion before revascularization.	Modern approach: If hard signs of ischemia (absent pulse, expanding hematoma) -&gt; OR. If soft signs or ABI &lt; 0.9 -&gt; CTA. The key selects Arteriography.	Step 2 CK: Posterior knee dislocation + Pulse deficit = Popliteal artery injury until proven otherwise.				> surgery Vascular
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Lymphatics	P4, Q27. A healthy 65-year old male was fowld on routine examination to have a symptomless central abdominal pulsatile mass. Ultrasound is the screening modality of choice for Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm (AAA). It is non-invasive, inexpensive, and highly accurate for measuring diameter.	Screening indicated for men 65-75 who have ever smoked.	Step 2 CK: Diagnosis and monitoring of AAA is by Ultrasound; Pre-operative planning is by CT Angio.				> surgery Vascular
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Lymphatics	P5, Q28. An asymptomatic infrarenal abdominal aneurysm measuring 8 cm in diameter was discovered in a 70-year-old male who was otherwise healthy. The proper management is by:	Strict limitation of activity.	Reassurance and regular check-ups.	Antihypertensive medication.	Elective aneurysm resection.	Resection when symptoms develop.	D	Elective aneurysm resection.	The threshold for elective repair of AAA is 5.5 cm in men (or 5.0 cm in women). An 8 cm aneurysm carries a massive risk of rupture (20-40% per year) that far outweighs surgical risk. Immediate elective repair is indicated.	Risk of rupture increases exponentially with diameter.	Step 2 CK: Symptomatic AAA (pain/tender) requires Urgent/Emergent repair regardless of size.				> surgery Vascular

Critical Limb Ischemia = Rest pain (Nocturnal) or Tissue Loss (Ulcer/Gangrene).	Step 2 CK: First-line for claudication is a supervised walking program.				> surgery Vascular

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q2. About 3rd degree (full thickness) burn:	Completely painless	No graft is needed	Healing occurs from skin appendages	Usually dry, white or black		D	Usually dry, white or black	Full-thickness (3rd degree) burns destroy the entire dermis and are characterized by a leathery, dry, white, or black (eschar) appearance. While they are insensate (painless), the appearance is the primary descriptive feature in the options. (Note: They typically require grafting, unlike partial burns). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q5. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q6. About healing intentions:	Ulcers heal by first intention	Second intention gives better cosmoses than first	First intention occurs on clean cut edges without gaps or tissue loss	All of the above		C	First intention occurs on clean cut edges without gaps or tissue loss	Healing by first intention (primary union) occurs in clean, approximated wounds (sutured incisions) with minimal scar. Second intention involves granulation and contraction (open wounds), leading to worse cosmesis.	Ulcers heal by second intention.	Step 1: First intention: Neutrophils -&gt; Macrophages -&gt; Fibroblasts -&gt; Collagen (Type III -&gt; Type I).				> Healing surgery Wound

Step 1: Vitamin C deficiency (Scurvy) impairs hydroxylation of proline/lysine -&gt; poor collagen cross-linking -&gt; wound dehiscence.				> Healing surgery Wound

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P2, Q13. All of the following are signs of epitheliomatous transformation in basal cell carcinoma except:	Rapid growth	Invasion of basement membrane	Everted edges	Hard fixed LNs		B	Invasion of basement membrane	"This question refers to signs suggesting transformation to a more aggressive state (or misdiagnosis of SCC). Invasion of the basement membrane is a *definition* of any carcinoma (including standard BCC), not a specific sign of &#x27;transformation&#x27; or high-grade behavior like rapid growth or nodal metastasis. However, clinically, BCCs are locally invasive but rarely metastasize. Hard LNs and everted edges are signs of SCC. The question likely frames &#x27;epitheliomatous transformation&#x27; as clinical dedifferentiation or Marjolin&#x27;s change, which makes &#x27;microscopic invasion&#x27; a non-clinical/distinct sign in this context."	Key concept: Clinical signs of aggression vs histologic definition.	Step 1: Basement membrane invasion distinguishes carcinoma in situ from invasive carcinoma.				> Skin surgery Tumors
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P2, Q14. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P5, Q34. Administration of colloids.	Blood transfusion.	A	Maintaining an hourly urine output between 30 and 50 ml. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q2. About 3rd degree (full thickness) burn:	Completely painless	No graft is needed	Healing occurs from skin appendages	Usually dry, white or black		D	Usually dry, white or black	Full-thickness (3rd degree) burns destroy the entire dermis and are characterized by a leathery, dry, white, or black (eschar) appearance. While they are insensate (painless), the appearance is the primary descriptive feature in the options. (Note: They typically require grafting, unlike partial burns).	Third-degree burns have no remaining skin appendages to regenerate from; healing is by contraction or grafting.	Step 2 CK: Painless burn = 3rd degree (nerve endings destroyed). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	"P1, Q3. The head/neck is 9%, and the front of the trunk (chest + abdomen) is 18%.	Adult Rule of Nines: Head 9%, Arm 9%, Leg 18%, Chest 18%, Back 18%, Genitals 1%. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q5. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q6. About healing intentions:	Ulcers heal by first intention	Second intention gives better cosmoses than first	First intention occurs on clean cut edges without gaps or tissue loss	All of the above		C	First intention occurs on clean cut edges without gaps or tissue loss	Healing by first intention (primary union) occurs in clean, approximated wounds (sutured incisions) with minimal scar. Second intention involves granulation and contraction (open wounds), leading to worse cosmesis.	Ulcers heal by second intention. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P1, Q7. All of the followings are factors affecting wound healing:	Age of the patient	General condition	Corticosteroid	Type of wound and type of closure	All of the above.	E	All of the above. Corticosteroids inhibit inflammation and collagen synthesis, delaying healing.	Step 1: Vitamin C deficiency (Scurvy) impairs hydroxylation of proline/lysine -&gt; poor collagen cross-linking -&gt; wound dehiscence.				> Healing surgery Wound

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P2, Q13. All of the following are signs of epitheliomatous transformation in basal cell carcinoma except:	Rapid growth	Invasion of basement membrane	Everted edges	Hard fixed LNs		B	Invasion of basement membrane	"This question refers to signs suggesting transformation to a more aggressive state (or misdiagnosis of SCC). Invasion of the basement membrane is a *definition* of any carcinoma (including standard BCC), not a specific sign of &#x27;transformation&#x27; or high-grade behavior like rapid growth or nodal metastasis. However, clinically, BCCs are locally invasive but rarely metastasize. Hard LNs and everted edges are signs of SCC. The question likely frames &#x27;epitheliomatous transformation&#x27; as clinical dedifferentiation or Marjolin&#x27;s change, which makes &#x27;microscopic invasion&#x27; a non-clinical/distinct sign in this context."	Key concept: Clinical signs of aggression vs histologic definition.	Step 1: Basement membrane invasion distinguishes carcinoma in situ from invasive carcinoma.				> Skin surgery Tumors
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P2, Q14. The following type of malignant melanoma has the best prognosis:	Superficial spreading type	Amelanotic melanoma	Acral type	Nodular melanoma		A	Superficial spreading type	Superficial spreading melanoma has a prolonged radial growth phase before vertical invasion, generally conferring a better prognosis compared to Nodular (rapid vertical growth) or Acral/Mucosal types. (Note: Lentigo Maligna has the absolute best, but among these, SSM is better than Nodular/Acral).	Nodular melanoma has the worst prognosis due to early vertical growth.	Step 2 CK: ABCD rule: Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter (&gt;6mm).				> Skin surgery Tumors
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P3, Q15. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P5, Q30. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P5, Q31. Wound healing is not impaired by	Anemia.	Hypoproteinemia.	Ascorbic acid deficiency.	Cortisone administration.	Immunosuppressive therapy.	A	Anemia.	Mild to moderate anemia does not significantly impair wound healing as long as blood volume and oxygenation are adequate. Hypoproteinemia (no building blocks), Scurvy (no collagen crosslink), and Steroids/Immunosuppressants significantly impair healing.	Oxygen tension is more critical than Hematocrit. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P5, Q32. Metabolic changes after burning are due to the following except:	The endocrine responses to injury. "Burns cause *increased* heat loss (hypermetabolism) due to loss of the skin barrier and evaporation. The metabolic rate rises to generate heat. Therefore, &#x27;Reduced heat loss&#x27; is the incorrect statement."	Hypermetabolic response: Tachycardia, Hyperthermia, Catabolism.	Step 2 CK: Keep burn patients in a warm environment to reduce metabolic demand.				> Burns surgery

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Plastic surgery	P5, Q34. Administration of colloids.	Blood transfusion.	A	Maintaining an hourly urine output between 30 and 50 ml. It is *not* indicated for all asymptomatic cases.	Many men have varicoceles and are fertile.	Step 2 CK: Grade 3 (visible) varicocele has the highest impact on fertility.				> surgery Testis

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Testis	P5, Q34. Size fluctuation is a character of:	Infantile hydrocele.	Congenital hydrocele.	Both A and B.	None of the above.		B	Congenital hydrocele. D	Encysted hydrocele of the cord.	The traction test involves pulling the testis downward. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Venous	P1, Q1. Pressure in superficial veins of the leg during stranding:	60 mmHg	70 mmHg	80 mmHg	90 mmHg		C	80 mmHg	The venous pressure in the superficial veins of the leg (e.g., at the ankle) in a standing (stranding) position is approximately 80-90 mmHg due to the hydrostatic column of blood from the heart to the foot. Step 1: Venous valves prevent retrograde flow; their failure leads to varicose veins and venous hypertension.				> surgery Venous

Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Venous	P6, Q39. The hemodynamic effects of arteriovenous include the following except:	Decreased peripheral resistance.	Reduced cardiac output.	Increased venous pressure.	Reduced diastolic pressure.	Increased heart size.	B	Reduced cardiac output.	An Arteriovenous (AV) fistula shunts blood from high-pressure arteries to low-pressure veins, bypassing arterioles. This decreases peripheral resistance and increases venous return, leading to *Increased* Cardiac Output (High Output State), not reduced.	Wide pulse pressure is characteristic. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Venous	P6, Q41. The proper treatment of superficial thrombophlebitis is:	Walking with elastic stockings.	Antibiotics.	Clot dissolvers.	Anticoagulants.	Venoligation.	A	Walking with elastic stockings.	Superficial thrombophlebitis is usually self-limiting. Treatment is symptomatic: NSAIDs, Compression stockings, and Ambulation. Anticoagulation is reserved for cases near the saphenofemoral junction.	Septic thrombophlebitis requires antibiotics and excision.	Step 2 CK: If it involves the Saphenofemoral junction, treat as DVT.				> surgery Venous
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Venous	P6, Q42. Post-operative deep venous thrombosis is suspected from the following except:	Unexplained postoperative fever or tachycardia.	Pain in the sole or calf.	Swelling or edema of the calf or leg.	Pain on plantar flexion of the foot.	Color changes in the skin of the leg.	D	Pain on plantar flexion of the foot.	"Homan&#x27;s sign describes calf pain on *dorsiflexion* of the foot (stretching the calf muscles), not plantar flexion."	Unilateral calf swelling &gt; 3cm is a significant clinical sign.	Step 2 CK: Modified Wells Criteria is the best clinical prediction tool.				> surgery Venous
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Venous	P6, Q43. Which statement is untrue concerning pulmonary embolism:	It is usually a complication of deep venous thrombosis.	May cause sudden death.	May occur without obvious signs and symptoms in the legs.	Can be readily diagnosed by X-ray, examination of the chest.	Requires immediate heparinization.	D	Can be readily diagnosed by X-ray, examination of the chest.	"Chest X-ray is insensitive for PE. It is often normal or shows non-specific findings (atelectasis). Classic signs like Westermark sign or Hampton&#x27;s hump are rare. It cannot be &#x27;readily diagnosed&#x27; by CXR alone."	Normal CXR in a hypoxemic patient suggests PE. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::MCQ المطري محدث::Venous	P7, Q44. The commonest cause of fatal pulmonary embolism is	Iliac vein thrombosis.	Thrombophlebitis of the femoral vein.	Calf vein thrombosis.	Axillary vein thrombosis.	Phlebitis migrans.	A	Iliac vein thrombosis.	Thrombi in larger, proximal veins (Iliac, Femoral) are larger in caliber and cause more hemodynamically significant (fatal) emboli than distal calf thrombi. Iliac thrombosis is often asymptomatic until PE occurs.	Calf DVT rarely causes fatal PE but can propagate proximally.	Step 1: Saddle embolus blocks both pulmonary arteries and causes sudden death.				> surgery Venous
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P1, Q1. One of the following statements as regard investigations for breast cancer is true	Fifty per cent of breast cancers are missed by population-based mammographic screening.	Ultrasound can also be used as a screening tool.	Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) can be a useful imaging tool.	Fine-needle aspiration cytology (FNAC) and core biopsy are equally useful diagnostically.		C	Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) can be a useful imaging tool.	MRI is a highly sensitive imaging tool used for screening high-risk patients (e.g., BRCA carriers) and for evaluating extent of disease. It is more sensitive than mammography but less specific.	Breast MRI is indicated for screening in women with &gt;20% lifetime risk of breast cancer.	Step 2 CK: MRI is the most sensitive test for invasive breast cancer but is not used for routine screening due to cost and false positives.	Breast &gt; Imaging			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P1, Q2. Lipomas can occur in the breast but are relatively rare compared to other benign conditions like fibrocystic changes or fibroadenomas. The statement that they are a 'common' condition is the false one in this list.	Most common benign breast mass in women &lt;30 is Fibroadenoma. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P1, Q3. Which of the following conditions have an increased risk of breast carcinoma	Breast cyst	Duct ectasia	Atypical ductal or lobular hyperplasia	Fibroadenoma.		C	Atypical ductal or lobular hyperplasia	Atypical hyperplasia (ductal or lobular) significantly increases the risk of developing invasive breast cancer (4-5x relative risk). Non-proliferative lesions like simple cysts and duct ectasia do not carry increased risk.	Relative Risk of Breast Cancer: Atypical Hyperplasia = 4-5x; LCIS/DCIS = 8-10x. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P1, Q4. In breast carcinoma, which one of the following statements is false?	Ductal carcinoma is the most common variant.	Lobular carcinoma occurs in 15 per cent.	There may be a combination of lobular and ductal features.	Colloid, medullary and tubular carcinomas carry a poor prognosis.		D	Colloid, medullary and tubular carcinomas carry a poor prognosis.	Colloid (mucinous), medullary, and tubular carcinomas are special histologic subtypes that generally carry a *better* prognosis compared to invasive ductal carcinoma not otherwise specified.	Tubular carcinoma has an excellent prognosis with &gt;95% survival.	Step 1: Medullary carcinoma is often associated with BRCA1 mutations and has a lymphocytic infiltrate.	Breast &gt; Pathology			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P2, Q5. In the treatment of breast cancer, which of the following statements are false?	There is a higher rate of local recurrence after conservative surgery and radiotherapy.	After mastectomy, radiotherapy to the chest wall is not indicated.	Sentinel lymph node biopsy should be done in clinically node-negative disease.	Besides treating the patient, the role of axillary surgery is to stage the patient accurately.		B	After mastectomy, radiotherapy to the chest wall is not indicated.	Post-mastectomy radiation *is* indicated for patients with high-risk features, such as tumors &gt;5 cm, positive margins, or &gt;4 positive lymph nodes. Therefore, statement B is false.	Breast Conservation Therapy (Lumpectomy + Radiation) has equivalent overall survival to Mastectomy.	Step 2 CK: Sentinel Lymph Node Biopsy is the standard of care for staging the axilla in clinically node-negative patients.	Breast &gt; Management

Step 2 CK: Fibroadenomas are the most common benign tumor in young women.	Breast &gt; Benign Conditions			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	"P10, Q38. Which of the following conditions increases a woman&#x27;s risk of breast cancer"	Sclerosing adenosis	Fibroadenoma	Atypical lobular hyperplasia	Intraductal papilloma		c	Atypical lobular hyperplasia	Atypical Lobular Hyperplasia (ALH) is a proliferative lesion with atypia that significantly increases the risk of breast cancer in either breast. Sclerosing adenosis and simple fibroadenomas have minimal to no increased risk.	Relative Risk ~4-5x.	Step 2 CK: Atypia on core biopsy is an indication for excisional biopsy to rule out malignancy. Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P10, Q39. A 35 year old woman with BRAC-1 gene mutation seeks your advice about her known increased risk of breast cancer. None of the above.		c	Prophylactic bilateral mastectomy and if she has completed childbearing, prophylactic bilateral oophorectomy.	For BRCA1 carriers, prophylactic bilateral mastectomy and salpingo-oophorectomy (after childbearing) provide the greatest risk reduction (up to 90-95%).	BRCA1 is also associated with Ovarian carcinoma.	Step 2 CK: Screening for BRCA carriers includes annual MRI starting at age 25 and Mammography at age 30.	Breast &gt; Management			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 1	P10, Q40. It is often multifocal and bilateral.	LCIS is a marker of increased risk, not a direct precursor obligate to cancer.	Step 1: LCIS cells lack E-cadherin expression.	Breast &gt; Pathology			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 2	P1, Q41. Moderate ductal hyperplasia of the breast is characterized by the microscopic finding of	3-4 cell layer above the basement membrane	5 or more cell layers above the basement membrane	Obstruction of &gt;50% of the ductal lumen by hyperplastic cells	Obstruction of &gt;70%of the ductal lumen by hyperplastic cells.		B	5 or more cell layers above the basement membrane	Moderate to florid ductal hyperplasia is typically characterized by an increase in cell layers (usually &gt;4) distending the ducts. Mild hyperplasia is 2-4 layers.	Ductal Hyperplasia is part of 'Proliferative Breast Disease without Atypia' (Risk 1.5-2x). Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 2	P1, Q42. Which of the following is appropriate treatment for 3 cm fibroadenoma	Resection	Cryo-ablation	Observation	All of the above.		D	All of the above.	Management of a 3 cm fibroadenoma can vary based on patient age and preference. Options include observation (if stable/young), resection (if growing/anxious), or cryoablation.	"Fibroadenomas &gt;3-4 cm are sometimes called &#x27;Giant Fibroadenomas&#x27; and are usually excised."	Step 2 CK: In women &lt;30, observation is acceptable for fibroadenomas if the diagnosis is confirmed by imaging/biopsy and the lesion is stable.	Breast &gt; Management			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 2	P1, Q43. All the following are recognized side effects of radiotherapy except:	Local burn.	Pulmonary fibrosis.	End arteritis.	None of the above.		D	None of the above.	"Radiotherapy to the breast/chest wall can cause acute skin reactions (burns), late pulmonary fibrosis, and endarteritis (vascular damage). Since all listed are known side effects, &#x27;None of the above&#x27; is the exception indicating there are no false items in the list."	Angiosarcoma is a rare but serious late complication of breast radiation (Stewart-Treves is lymphangiosarcoma from lymphedema, but radiation also causes sarcomas).	Step 2 CK: Radiation pneumonitis presents with cough and dyspnea weeks to months after therapy.	Breast &gt; Complications			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 2	P1, Q44. Adjuvant chemotherapy is indicated in:	+ve LN biopsy.	-ve hormonal receptors.	Poor prognosis of the case.	All of the above.		D	All of the above.	Adjuvant chemotherapy is generally indicated for high-risk features: node-positive disease, hormone receptor-negative (Triple Negative/HER2+), and large or high-grade tumors (poor prognosis).	Oncotype DX testing helps determine the benefit of chemotherapy in ER+ node-negative patients.	Step 2 CK: Chemotherapy is standard for Node (+) or Triple Negative breast cancer.	Breast &gt; Management			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 2	P2, Q45. Chemotherapy is the primary palliative treatment in the following conditions:	Visceral metastasis.	Advanced case in premenopausal women.	-ve hormonal receptors with distant metastasis.	All of the above.		D	All of the above.	Systemic chemotherapy is the mainstay of treatment for metastatic (Stage IV) disease, particularly with visceral crisis or hormone receptor-negative status.	Hormonal therapy is preferred for ER+ metastatic disease *without* visceral crisis.	Step 2 CK: Visceral crisis (lung lymphangitic spread, liver dysfunction) mandates chemotherapy over hormonal therapy for rapid response.	Breast &gt; Oncology			
Surgery MCQ Raihana Med::المنصورة محدث::Breast part 2	P2, Q46.
